info.title: Hostinger Email API
info.description:
# Overview The Hostinger Email API provides a set of endpoints that allow developers to manage Hostinger Email programmatically. This API enables you to manage mailboxes and related resources tied to your Hostinger account. The API is (mostly) RESTful and uses standard HTTP methods and status codes. # Authentication The API uses tokens for authentication. To authenticate your requests, you need to include a valid bearer token in the `Authorization` header of your HTTP requests: ```yaml Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_TOKEN ``` API tokens can be created and managed from the email provisioning tab in the Hostinger Panel. Tokens are scoped to a single order; their permissions are limited to that order. Only mailboxes belonging to the order can be accessed and managed by an API token. # Rate Limiting To ensure fair usage and prevent abuse, the API enforces rate limits on the number of requests that can be made within a certain time period. If you exceed the rate limit, you will receive a `429 Too Many Requests` response. Your IP address might get temporarily blocked if you exceed the rate limit multiple times. # Parameters All requests sent to the API must have the content type `application/json`. `POST`, `PUT`, `PATCH` methods may include a JSON object in the request body. Documentation provides the required structure and examples of the object. Some endpoints require path parameters. These parameters are included in the URL path and are marked with curly braces. # Response envelope All non-`204` responses wrap the payload in a top-level `data` field. Single resource: ```json { "data": { "resourceId": "AC1a2b3c4d5e6f7g", "address": "user@example.com" } } ``` Collection (empty or otherwise): ```json { "data": [] } ``` # Pagination Paginated responses include a top-level `pagination` object alongside `data`. By default, the API returns 50 items per page. The page number can be specified using the `page` query parameter, for example: `/core/v1/public/mailboxes?page=2`. ```json { "data": [], "pagination": { "page": 1, "perPage": 25, "total": 137, "totalPages": 6 } } ``` # Errors All `4xx` and `5xx` responses share a single envelope. Always parse `code` for programmatic handling; `error` is a human-readable message intended for logs and support tickets. ```json { "error": "Mailbox not found.", "code": "ERR_MAILBOX_NOT_FOUND", "params": {} } ``` # AI agents (MCP) Want to read or send your email from Claude, Cursor, ChatGPT, or any other AI assistant? Hostinger ships an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that wraps this API: ``` https://mcp.mail.hostinger.com/mcp ``` Use the same API token from the Hostinger Panel. Your agent only reaches what the token permits — order-scoped, optionally restricted to specific mailboxes within the order.
Claude Code (CLI) ```bash claude mcp add --scope user --transport http \ --header "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_HOSTINGER_API_TOKEN" \ -- hostinger-email https://mcp.mail.hostinger.com/mcp ```
Claude Desktop Claude Desktop connects to MCP servers via stdio. Use the `mcp-remote` bridge to talk to our HTTP server. Requires Node.js (`npx` ships with it). Edit the Claude Desktop config file. Path depends on your OS: - **macOS:** `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json` - **Windows:** `%APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json` - **Linux:** `~/.config/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json` Set the contents to: ```json { "mcpServers": { "hostinger-email": { "command": "npx", "args": [ "-y", "mcp-remote", "https://mcp.mail.hostinger.com/mcp", "--header", "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_HOSTINGER_API_TOKEN" ] } } } ``` Quit Claude Desktop fully (not just close the window) and reopen it. The connector icon should appear in the message composer. If you have Claude Desktop's paid "Custom Connectors" feature, you can skip the `mcp-remote` bridge entirely and add the HTTP URL directly via the in-app UI: ```json { "mcpServers": { "hostinger-email": { "type": "http", "url": "https://mcp.mail.hostinger.com/mcp", "headers": { "Authorization": "Bearer YOUR_HOSTINGER_API_TOKEN" } } } } ```
Cursor Create or edit `~/.cursor/mcp.json` (global) or `.cursor/mcp.json` in your project root: ```json { "mcpServers": { "hostinger-email": { "url": "https://mcp.mail.hostinger.com/mcp", "headers": { "Authorization": "Bearer YOUR_HOSTINGER_API_TOKEN" } } } } ``` Restart Cursor. The server is also configurable via `Cmd-Shift-P` → `View: Open MCP Settings` (newer Cursor versions).
What can the agent do? Same as this REST API — read, search, send, move, flag, delete messages; list folders and mailboxes; manage webhooks. The agent calls these endpoints under the hood using your API token. The agent only reaches what your token permits. Tokens are scoped to a single Hostinger order, and you can further restrict each token to specific mailboxes within that order when generating it in the Panel.

get.description: List messages in a folder. Use POST /search for filtering. Sort fields: uid, date, size (prefix with `-` for descending). Default `-uid`.

parameters.name: mailboxResourceId
parameters.description: Resource ID of the managed mailbox the bearer token is authorized for.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort field with optional `-` prefix for descending. Allowed: uid, date, size.

delete.description: Permanently delete every message in a folder (empty the folder).

parameters.name: mailboxResourceId
parameters.description: Resource ID of the managed mailbox the bearer token is authorized for.

get.description: Retrieve a single message by UID.

parameters.name: mailboxResourceId
parameters.description: Resource ID of the managed mailbox the bearer token is authorized for.

parameters.name: mailboxResourceId
parameters.description: Resource ID of the managed mailbox the bearer token is authorized for.

patch.description: Add and/or remove flags on a single message. Returns the updated message.

parameters.name: mailboxResourceId
parameters.description: Resource ID of the managed mailbox the bearer token is authorized for.

post.description: Permanently delete multiple messages from a folder.

parameters.name: mailboxResourceId
parameters.description: Resource ID of the managed mailbox the bearer token is authorized for.

parameters.name: mailboxResourceId
parameters.description: Resource ID of the managed mailbox the bearer token is authorized for.

parameters.name: attachmentId
parameters.description: Attachment ID (opaque, returned by GET message).

get.description: Retrieve raw RFC822 source of a message as `message/rfc822` attachment.

parameters.name: mailboxResourceId
parameters.description: Resource ID of the managed mailbox the bearer token is authorized for.

get.description: Retrieve rendered text (plain + HTML) of a message. Marks message as `\Seen`.

parameters.name: mailboxResourceId
parameters.description: Resource ID of the managed mailbox the bearer token is authorized for.

post.description: Move a single message to a target folder.

parameters.name: mailboxResourceId
parameters.description: Resource ID of the managed mailbox the bearer token is authorized for.

post.description: Move multiple messages from a source folder to a target folder.

parameters.name: mailboxResourceId
parameters.description: Resource ID of the managed mailbox the bearer token is authorized for.

post.description: Search messages in a folder. Filters in body; pagination and sort via query (`page`, `perPage`, `sort`).

parameters.name: mailboxResourceId
parameters.description: Resource ID of the managed mailbox the bearer token is authorized for.

post.description: Add and/or remove flags on multiple messages. Returns 200 when all UIDs succeed, 207 with per-UID outcome when some fail.

parameters.name: mailboxResourceId
parameters.description: Resource ID of the managed mailbox the bearer token is authorized for.

207.description: Some UIDs failed; see body for per-UID outcome.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of folders in the managed mailbox.

parameters.name: mailboxResourceId
parameters.description: Resource ID of the managed mailbox the bearer token is authorized for.

post.description: Create a new folder in the managed mailbox.

parameters.name: mailboxResourceId
parameters.description: Resource ID of the managed mailbox the bearer token is authorized for.

parameters.name: mailboxResourceId
parameters.description: Resource ID of the managed mailbox the bearer token is authorized for.

delete.description: Delete a folder and all of its subfolders.

parameters.name: mailboxResourceId
parameters.description: Resource ID of the managed mailbox the bearer token is authorized for.

get.description: Returns the authenticated account and the mailboxes it can manage.

200.description: Authenticated account with the mailboxes the API key can manage.

get.description: Retrieve storage and message quota usage for the managed mailbox.

parameters.name: mailboxResourceId
parameters.description: Resource ID of the managed mailbox the bearer token is authorized for.

post.description: Send a message from the managed mailbox. Saves a copy to INBOX.Sent.

parameters.name: mailboxResourceId
parameters.description: Resource ID of the managed mailbox the bearer token is authorized for.

204.description: Message sent and saved to sent folder.

parameters.name: mailboxResourceId
parameters.description: Resource ID of the managed mailbox the bearer token is authorized for.

post.description: Create a webhook. The response includes the one-time `secret` — store it securely as it is never returned again.

parameters.name: mailboxResourceId
parameters.description: Resource ID of the managed mailbox the bearer token is authorized for.

get.description: Retrieve a single webhook by id.

parameters.name: mailboxResourceId
parameters.description: Resource ID of the managed mailbox.

parameters.name: mailboxResourceId
parameters.description: Resource ID of the managed mailbox.

parameters.name: mailboxResourceId
parameters.description: Resource ID of the managed mailbox.

post.description: Regenerate the webhook secret. The previous secret is immediately invalidated. The new secret is returned once.

parameters.name: mailboxResourceId
parameters.description: Resource ID of the managed mailbox.

post.description: Send a test delivery to the webhook URL and return the upstream response.

parameters.name: mailboxResourceId
parameters.description: Resource ID of the managed mailbox.

Error.description: Standard error envelope. Frontend translations key off `code`, never off `error`.

params.description: Additional structured context for the error. May be omitted.

total.description: Total number of items across all pages.

V1.FolderMessages.DeleteBulkRequest.description: Body for permanently deleting multiple messages.

uids.description: Message UIDs to delete. 1-100 entries, each > 0.

V1.FolderMessages.FlagsBulkRequest.description: Add and/or remove flags on multiple messages. At least one of addFlags or removeFlags must be set.

uids.description: Message UIDs to update. 1-100 entries, each > 0.

V1.FolderMessages.FlagsRequest.description: Add and/or remove flags on a message. At least one of addFlags or removeFlags must be set.

V1.FolderMessages.Message.description: A message in a folder of the managed mailbox.

V1.FolderMessages.MessageAddress.description: A single email address with optional display name.

V1.FolderMessages.MessageAttachment.description: Attachment metadata attached to a message.

id.description: Opaque attachment identifier. Use as the {attachmentId} path parameter on the download endpoint.

V1.FolderMessages.MessageText.description: Rendered text and HTML parts of a message.

V1.FolderMessages.MoveBulkRequest.description: Body for moving multiple messages to a target folder.

uids.description: Message UIDs to move. 1-100 entries, each > 0.

V1.FolderMessages.MoveRequest.description: Body for moving a single message to a target folder.

V1.FolderMessages.SearchRequest.description: Search criteria. All fields optional; combine to narrow results.

V1.FolderMessages.UpdateFlagsResult.description: Per-UID outcome of a bulk flag update. 200 when every UID succeeded, 207 when at least one failed.

V1.Folders.Folder.description: A folder (IMAP mailbox) in the managed mailbox.

path.description: Full hierarchical folder path including the delimiter.

delimiter.description: Hierarchy delimiter used by the IMAP server.

specialUse.description: IMAP SPECIAL-USE attribute (RFC 6154). `null` for regular folders.

messageCount.description: Total number of messages in the folder.

unreadCount.description: Number of unread messages in the folder.

name.description: New folder name. Length 1-100 after trimming.

V1.Me.Mailbox.description: A mailbox the authenticated API token can manage.

orderResourceId.description: Identifier of the order this API token is scoped to.

mailboxes.description: Mailboxes that the authenticated API token can manage.

V1.Quota.Quota.description: Storage and message quota usage for the managed mailbox.

quotas.description: Per-resource quota breakdown reported by the IMAP server.

totalUsage.description: Aggregate storage usage across all resources, in bytes.

totalLimit.description: Aggregate storage limit across all resources, in bytes.

totalPercentage.description: Aggregate storage usage as a percentage of the total limit.

supported.description: Whether the IMAP server reports quota information for this mailbox.

V1.Quota.QuotaResource.description: Usage and limit for a single IMAP quota resource.

resourceName.description: Name of the quota resource as reported by the IMAP server.

usage.description: Current usage of the resource (bytes for STORAGE, count for MESSAGE).

limit.description: Maximum allowed value for the resource (bytes for STORAGE, count for MESSAGE).

percentage.description: Usage as a percentage of the limit.

V1.Send.Attachment.description: Attachment payload for outgoing mail. Supports regular attachments and inline images linked via Content-ID.

content.description: Attachment body. Base64-encoded by default; set encoding to switch.

cid.description: Content-ID for inline images. Reference from HTML as .

encoding.description: Encoding of content. Defaults to base64.

V1.Send.Request.description: Outgoing message payload. At least one of to, cc, or bcc must be present.

V1.Webhooks.ResourceWithSecret.description: Single webhook payload including the one-time secret.

V1.Webhooks.TestResult.description: Result of a webhook test delivery.

V1.Webhooks.UpdateRequest.description: Body for partially updating a webhook. All fields optional; only present fields are applied.

V1.Webhooks.Webhook.description: Webhook configured for a managed mailbox.

accountResourceId.description: Resource ID of the mailbox this webhook is attached to.

description.example: Notifies our CRM when a new email arrives

V1.Webhooks.WebhookWithSecret.description: Webhook payload that includes the one-time `secret`. Returned at creation and after secret regeneration. The secret is delivered as a bearer token on every webhook request (`Authorization: Bearer <secret>`); store it securely as it is not returned again.

Common.Response.BadGatewayResponse.description: Upstream service is unavailable or returned an unexpected response.

Common.Response.ForbiddenResponse.description: Token is not authorized to manage the requested mailbox.

Common.Response.ValidationFailedResponse.description: Request payload failed validation. `params` maps field name to an array of error messages.

BearerAuth.description: Bearer authentication using API tokens in the Authorization header.

tags.name: Account
tags.description: Inspect the authenticated account and the mailboxes it can manage.

tags.name: Folders
tags.description: Manage IMAP folders inside a managed mailbox.

tags.name: Messages
tags.description: Read, search, and manage messages inside a managed mailbox.

tags.name: Send
tags.description: Send mail through the managed mailbox.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Manage webhooks for a managed mailbox.

tags.name: Quota
tags.description: Inspect storage and message quota usage for a managed mailbox.

x-mcp-instructions:
Hostinger Email API access — use this server when the user wants to read, search, send, organize, or manage email in their Hostinger mailbox(es), or manage email webhooks.

Capabilities:
- List mailboxes belonging to the authenticated Hostinger order
- List folders inside a mailbox (Inbox, Sent, Drafts, Trash, custom)
- List, search, fetch, move, delete, flag messages
- Read message body (text/HTML), full RFC822 source, attachments
- Send email from a mailbox
- Manage webhooks (create, list, test, regenerate secret, delete)

Mailbox discovery: there is no standalone "list mailboxes" endpoint. Call `GET /api/v1/me` first — the response contains `data.mailboxes[]` with each mailbox's `resourceId` and `address`. Use that `resourceId` as `mailboxResourceId` in every other endpoint.

Workflow:
1. Discover endpoints (optionally filter by tag: "Messages", "Mailboxes", "Folders", "Webhooks", "Send").
2. Inspect the operation schema (parameters, request body, responses).
3. Execute the call. Path templates use `{placeholder}` substitution.

Auth: each request must carry `Authorization: Bearer <customer-Hostinger-token>`. Tokens are scoped to one Hostinger order and may be further restricted to specific mailboxes within that order — assume access is partial unless verified.
